d: The project at a glance — life timeline, surname trail, candidate funnel, and Hessian parish map. Visual entry points to the full research narrative.
b: Visualize — Charles Research Skip to main content ← Charles Research / Visualize Visualize The project at a glance. Four visual entry points before you read the prose: life timeline, surname trail, candidate funnel, parish map. Each diagram links into the relevant evidence narrative. A research site this dense rewards patient reading — but a first-time visitor often wants the shape of the argument before the argument itself. These five figures are the project's working diagrams. Each one stands alone; together they trace the case from the open question (where in Hesse?) to the working answer (the Trendelburg Konze pool), the gaps that remain, and the path the investigation itself took to get here. 1 · Life timeline · 1835–1903 Sixty-eight years across five chapters. Hover for detail; click any event to open its evidence card or section page. Open Charles in America → Open Civil War → Detailed life timeline page → 2 · Surname trail · the convergent-evidence argument Eight distinct American renderings across at least five scribes between 1866 and 1896 (per the 17 May 2026 rigor pass, expanding the canon from seven to eight with the Konzen z-form re-reading of the 1890 daughter's death record). Five of the eight cluster on the [ˈkɔn.sən] sound; one is the Hessian z-form (Konzen, 1890); two are vowel/onset mishearings (Gansen, Jansen). Konsen and Konzen together bracket the trail — two -en-preserving forms fifteen years apart, by two different scribes. Open Key Evidence Card 1 → The Konzen puzzle deep dive → 3 · Candidate funnel · 13 families investigated, 1 surviving hypothesis Two analytic filters — birth-year window and surname/parish congruence — eliminated all but the Trendelburg / Friedrichsfeld Konze pool of Churhessen . Each elimination is logged on its section page with the specific contradicting parish entry. Open Germany & Origins → What remains open → 4 · Hessian parish map · the geographic search Every parish within walking distance of Charles

t: Everyone in the Story — Charles Research
d: A unified index of every named person in the Charles Gannsen investigation: the immediate Gannsen-Kreichelt family, the seven children, the eleven witnesses who knew Charles in St. Louis, and the candidate-family subjects of the German-side search.
b: Everyone in the Story — Charles Research Skip to main content ← Charles Research / Everyone in the Story Everyone in the Story A single index of every named person in the investigation — the Gannsen-Kreichelt family, Charles's seven children, the eleven St. Louis witnesses, and the candidate-family subjects of the German-side search ~80 people · 5 categories · linkable to canonical profiles Filter All Immediate family Children Witnesses Candidate families Schräder charts — people shown Charles & the Gannsen-Kreichelt household Charles, his wife Auguste, and her immediate Kreichelt family in St. Louis. The Kreichelts converged with the Gannsens twice over: Auguste's father and brother both served in Battery B alongside Charles before the 1866 wedding. Charles Gannsen Subject b. 26 January 1835, Hesse-Cassel · d. 16 December 1903 † , St. Louis. Civil War sergeant, Battery B 2nd Missouri Light Artillery. Married Auguste Kreichelt 7 January 1866. → Charles in America tab Auguste Kreichelt Wife Charles's wife. Married at St. Marcus German Evangelical Church 7 January 1866 — eighteen days after his discharge. Mother of seven. FamilySearch LZKV-9F5 Georg Kreichelt Father-in-law Auguste's father. Battery B veteran. Walks his daughter down the aisle on 7 January 1866. → Civil War tab August Kreichelt Brother-in-law Auguste's brother. Battery B veteran. Sponsors Erwin Carl Konsen's 1875 baptism — the surviving Hesse-spelling of the family name. The seven children Each baptized at St. Marcus German Evangelical Church, St. Louis. Across the seven baptisms the surname spelling drifted through five distinct baptism-register renderings (Gonnson, Gansen, Gonsen, Jansen, Konsen) — the children's-baptism subset of the eight-attestation surname trail that is one of the central pieces of evidence in the surname-variant theory. → Open the Children index Friedrich Wilhelm Gonnson Child 1 1866–1867. Named for the deposed Elector of Hesse. Died in infancy. → Profile Maria Jos
